command.switchToTextEditor.title: Edit Raw Markdown
command.switchToPreview.title: Edit Rendered Markdown
config.codeBlockMaxHeight.description: Maximum display height of code blocks in pixels. A scrollbar appears when content exceeds this height.
config.codeBlockAutoConvert.description: Automatically convert supported code blocks, such as Mermaid, to a rich preview in the WYSIWYG editor.
config.codeBlockWordWrap.description: Controls word wrap for code blocks. By default, it follows VS Code's editor.wordWrap setting.
config.codeBlockWordWrap.on: Always wrap long lines in code blocks.
config.codeBlockWordWrap.off: Never wrap long lines in code blocks.
config.defaultMode.description: Default editing mode when opening a .md file.
config.defaultMode.preview: Open .md files with the WYSIWYG preview editor (default).
config.defaultMode.markdown: Open .md files with the VS Code text editor.
config.contentWidth.description: Width of the editor content area. Also available in the toolbar's typography (A) menu.
config.contentWidth.fixed: Cap at Max Content Width (below) and center.
config.maxContentWidth.description: Maximum content width in ch (character widths) when Content Width is 'fixed'. Because the unit is ch, the measure scales with the content font size.
config.tocContentGap.description: Left-side gap in pixels between the table of contents or its toggle button and the editor content. Default is 100.
config.tocPosition.description: Which side of the editor the table of contents docks on. Default is right.
config.frontmatterExpanded.description: Show the frontmatter (metadata) panel expanded when a file opens. Turn off to start collapsed; the panel's own Show/Hide metadata toggle still works either way.
config.tocPosition.left: Dock the table of contents on the left side of the editor.
config.tocPosition.right: Dock the table of contents on the right side of the editor.
config.tocAutoHideThreshold.description: Do not automatically show the table of contents when the document has this many or fewer non-empty headings. Manual opening is not affected. Default is 3.
config.customCss.description: Custom CSS files to load in the Markdown preview editor. Use absolute paths, workspace-relative paths, or ~/ paths.
config.customJs.description: Custom JavaScript files to load in the Markdown preview editor. Use absolute paths, workspace-relative paths, or ~/ paths.
config.fontPreset.description: Editor content font, also switchable from the toolbar font picker. 'editor' (the default) follows the VS Code editor font; the other presets use their Font Family Sans/Serif/Mono stack.
config.fontPreset.editor: Follow the VS Code editor font (editor.fontFamily).
config.fontPreset.serif: Serif stack (Iowan Old Style, Palatino, Georgia...).
config.fontFamilySans.description: Font-family stack used by the 'Sans serif' preset. Edit to define your own sans-serif stack.
config.fontFamilySerif.description: Font-family stack used by the 'Serif' preset. Edit to define your own serif stack.
config.fontFamilyMono.description: Font-family stack used by the 'Monospace' preset. Edit to define your own monospace stack.
config.fontSize.description: Content font size, as a percentage of the VS Code editor font size (50–200). Scales the rendered markdown and frontmatter only, not the surrounding UI; 100 matches the editor font size. Also adjustable from the toolbar font picker.
command.debugModeEnable.title: Debug Mode
command.debugModeDisable.title: ✓ Debug Mode
config.debugMode.description: Show debug overlays (e.g. table insert trigger zones). Intended for development use.
config.imageLocalPath.description: Local folder for saving images (relative or absolute path). Leave empty to auto-detect (images/, imgs/, assets/images/, assets/). Images are always stored locally and are never uploaded off your machine.
config.smartLinks.description: Resolve local links the way your site generator does: workspace-root paths, ancestor content roots (e.g. Hugo's content/), .md and index.md suffixes, and [[wikilink]] targets. When disabled, links resolve relative to the document only.
config.tableWrap.description: Controls how text wraps inside table cells. 'normal' avoids mid-word breaks (may cause narrower columns). 'aggressive' allows breaking long words to keep columns wider. 'none' disables wrapping entirely.
config.tableWrap.none: Do not wrap text in table cells. Long content will overflow horizontally.
config.tableWrap.normal: Wrap text at soft line breaks, but avoid breaking words in the middle. (Recommended default)
config.tableWrap.aggressive: Allow breaking words in the middle to prevent columns from becoming too narrow.
command.selectBlockHandles.title: Block Handles
config.blockHandles.description: Which block handles stay visible in the left gutter while you're not pointing at anything. Hovering a block always reveals its handle; this only controls the at-rest display. Also available from the toolbar's typography (A) menu.
config.blockHandles.headings: Keep heading level badges (H1–H6) visible; every other block's handle appears on hover. (Default)
config.blockHandles.always: Keep every block's handle visible at rest.
config.blockHandles.hover: Show no handles at rest — including headings. Everything appears on hover.
config.proofreading.enabled.description: Proofreading master switch: turns spelling, grammar, and style checking on or off all at once. When off, nothing is scanned or underlined and the whole feature is idle — your individual Spelling / Grammar / Style choices are preserved and come back when you turn it on. Toggle from the top of the Checks toolbar menu or the 'Toggle Proofreading' command.
config.styleCheck.enabled.description: Style check: flag fillers, redundancies, cliches, wordiness, passive voice, and AI-writing tells inline as you write (iA-Writer-style). Pick which checks run from the toolbar dropdown or the settings below. Also toggleable from the toolbar. (Requires the Proofreading master switch to be on.)
config.styleCheck.fillers.description: Style check: flag filler words (basically, really, very...).
config.styleCheck.redundancies.descripti: Style check: flag redundant phrases (end result, revert back...).
config.styleCheck.cliches.description: Style check: flag cliches (low-hanging fruit, moving forward...).
config.styleCheck.wordiness.description: Style check: flag wordy/expletive constructions (there is, the fact that...).
config.styleCheck.aiVocabulary.descripti: Style check (AI tell): flag over-reached vocabulary (delve, tapestry, leverage...).
config.styleCheck.aiArtifacts.descriptio: Style check (AI tell): flag leaked LLM boilerplate (as an AI, it's important to note...).
config.styleCheck.passive.description: Style check: flag passive voice (was written, is being reviewed) so you can lead with who acts. On by default; it can flag correct copular/locative English (was born, is located), so turn it off here if it gets noisy.
config.styleCheck.negativeParallelism.de: Style check (AI tell): flag the 'not just X, but Y' / 'it's not X, it's Y' cadence. On by default; it also catches the ordinary correlative 'not only X but also Y', so turn it off here if that bothers you.
config.styleCheck.longSentences.descript: Style check: flag sentences over 30 words.
config.styleCheck.ruleOfThree.descriptio: Style check (AI tell): flag three stacked adjectives (fast, cheap, and reliable).
config.styleCheck.emDash.description: Style check: flag em/en dash glyphs (prefer a spaced ASCII hyphen).
config.styleCheck.nonAsciiPunct.descript: Style check: flag curly quotes, the ellipsis glyph, and invisible spaces (normalize to ASCII).
config.spellCheck.enabled.description: Spelling: check English spelling with Harper (offline, on-device). Findings show as dotted underlines; click one for suggestions. Code, URLs, and identifiers are skipped. (Requires the Proofreading master switch to be on.)
config.grammarCheck.enabled.description: Grammar: surface Harper's non-spelling findings (repetition, word choice, grammar) as dotted underlines. Shares the offline Harper engine with spelling. (Requires the Proofreading master switch to be on.)
config.spellCheck.userWords.description: Your personal dictionary: words the spell check treats as correct. Managed via 'Add to dictionary' in the editor's suggestion popup.
config.styleCheck.exceptions.description: Phrases the style check must never flag, e.g. a filler word you use deliberately. Case-insensitive.
